About

Conservation work is hard. The tension between urgency, resources, and people is real. You don't need a perfect organization — you need one that can find its equilibrium and keep moving.

Culture is the ecosystem your team lives in every day.

T'Noya Thompson, PhD is a consultant and researcher working at the intersection of organizational leadership and conservation. Her practice centers on team and organizational culture: understanding how it forms, how it gets in the way, and how to intentionally build something better.

With a doctorate in Organizational Leadership and experience across field research, organizational assessment, and executive consulting, she helps conservation organizations understand and shape the cultures that either enable or constrain their work.

Photo: Claire Everett

“If we fail to understand our internal ecosystems, we risk not only losing people, but also our collective mission.” T’Noya Thompson, PhD

Services

Consulting and research for conservation organizations ready to invest in the cultures, teams, and leaders behind their mission.

Featured Offering

EcoCulture Assessment (ECA)

The first organizational culture assessment designed specifically for the conservation sector. Grounded in the Competing Values Framework (Cameron & Quinn, 2011), the ECA measures your organization across six dimensions — Ecosystem Climate, Ecosystem Leadership, Support Systems, Root Systems, Growth Pathways, and Vital Indicators — mapping your culture against four ecosystem types: Coral Reef, Rainforest, Savanna, and Desert.

The result is a clear picture of the culture you have, the culture your people want, and where the gap is costing you the most.

Get in touch about the EcoCulture Assessment →

01

Culture Assessment

Organizational and team culture assessment beyond the EcoCulture. Understand what you have, how it's affecting your work, and where to focus energy for lasting change.

02

Leadership Development

Coaching and consulting for conservation leaders navigating culture change, team dynamics, and organizational complexity. Individual and group formats available.

03

Team Development

Facilitation, structured workshops, and support for teams building the trust, communication, and shared language they need to do their most important work together.

IRB Approved  ·  Free to Participate

EcoCulture Assessment — 2026–2027 Validation Study

There is currently no validated culture assessment instrument designed specifically for conservation organizations. This study changes that.

Your organization participates for free. Staff complete the ECA anonymously online — about 10–15 minutes. In return, you receive a full aggregate EcoCulture Profile Summary: your ecosystem profile, culture gaps, and strategic recommendations. No cost. No strings.

  • Anonymous & confidential — no individual data collected
  • Results delivered within 6–8 weeks
  • Optional debrief call included
Express Your Interest →
Photo Credit: Running Wild Media

The Approach

Resilience is built through interconnectedness — in ecosystems and in organizations.

Every thriving ecosystem depends on symbiotic relationships, clear roles, and the conditions that let each part function well. Organizations work the same way. When something is out of equilibrium, the whole system feels the tension.

This practice draws on organizational research, applied leadership theory, and direct experience in the conservation sector to help teams and leaders see clearly and build purposefully.

Contact

Let's talk about your organization.

Whether you are navigating a culture challenge, building a new team, or just starting to name what's not working, reach out. A short conversation goes a long way.
